questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: safety / should / mines / our / be / in / priority / top
options: Safety in mines should be our top priority
In mines safety our top priority should be
Top priority safety should be in mines
Safety should be our top priority in mines
questions.solution: Safety should be our top priority in mines

The workers demanded better working conditions
Better working conditions the workers demanded
questions.solution: The workers demanded better working conditions

Worked together the team to solve the problem
Together the team worked to solve the problem
To solve the problem the team together worked
questions.solution: The team worked together to solve the problem

questions.stem: The Brayton cycle is the ideal cycle for a:
questions.solution: The Brayton (Joule) cycle models gas-turbine plants with constant-pressure heat addition.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Drilling — thrust and torque:
options: Thrust = 1.16·K·d·f^0.7. Torque = C·d²·f^0.8. d = drill diameter
questions.solution: Drill: point angle 118°. Thrust increases with feed, diameter. Peck drilling for deep holes.

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Cam follower dwell period:
options: Follower has zero velocity while cam rotates
questions.solution: Dwell: follower stationary while cam rotates.

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Friction angle relates to coefficient of friction by:
questions.solution: phi = arctan(mu). At impending motion, resultant at angle phi.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the simply supported beam shown (central load W = 10 kN, span L = 6 m), the maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: For a central point load, M_max = WL/4 = 10×6/4 = 15 kN·m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The maximum-shear-stress (Tresca) theory of failure compares the maximum shear stress with:
options: Half the yield stress in simple tension
questions.solution: Tresca predicts yielding when τ_max = σ_yield/2 (the shear at yield in a tensile test).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Detonation in CI engines:
options: Rapid uncontrolled combustion due to long ignition delay
questions.solution: Diesel knock: long ignition delay → more fuel accumulates → rapid combustion. Cetane number = ignition quality.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Regeneration in Rankine cycle:
options: Preheats feedwater using extracted steam (improves efficiency)
questions.solution: Regeneration: open or closed feedwater heaters. Reduces heat addition in boiler.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Arc welding uses:
options: Electric arc between electrode and workpiece to generate heat
questions.solution: Arc: intense heat (3000-5000°C). Electrode: consumable (SMAW, GMAW) or non-consumable (GTAW).

questions.stem: Fourier number Fo = αt/L² represents:
options: Ratio of conduction to heat storage

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A four-stroke engine completes one power stroke every:
questions.solution: Suction, compression, power and exhaust occupy two crankshaft revolutions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Nusselt number (Nu) is:
options: hL/k (ratio of convective to conductive heat transfer)
questions.solution: Nu = hL/k. Nu = 1 for pure conduction (stagnant fluid).

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Compression ratio of diesel engine is:
questions.solution: Diesel: higher CR for compression ignition.

options: ΔU = Q - W. Energy cannot be created or destroyed
questions.solution: Closed system. Sign convention: Q in +, W out +. Per unit mass: du = δq - δw.

questions.solution: Stoichiometric AFR: gasoline ≈ 14.7, diesel ≈ 14.5. Lean > stoichiometric, rich < stoichiometric.

questions.solution: τ_max = TR/J; J = πd⁴/32; τ = 943.14 MPa

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CFC refrigerants are being phased out due to:
questions.solution: CFCs destroy ozone layer. Replaced by HFCs (low ODP but high GWP) and natural refrigerants.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the tensile stress–strain curve shown for mild steel (yield ≈ 350 MPa), point A (end of the initial straight line) represents the:
questions.solution: Point A is the yield point where the linear elastic region ends; the peak (B) is the ultimate strength.
